reason: Fork boundary version must preserve migration ordering, migration gates, and user-file safety.
The update command owns project-versus-CLI comparison and migration application.
Existing npm self-upgrade behavior is being removed from the source-only fork.
Release tooling and public npm publishing assumptions change.
Focused tests must follow local Vitest isolation conventions.
Consumer upgrade coverage requires temporary-directory command integration tests.
Generated Python session scripts and source-oriented commands must remain cross-platform.
